enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request asking whether he would use teleportation if it destroyed the original and reconstructed a copy. He argues the soul is indestructible and inseparable from the self, so teleportation would preserve identity. He also connects teleportation to alien travel, predicts time travel as the next step, and argues that societal openness and trust — including open borders — must come before advanced technology can emerge.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: would use it — the soul is indestructible and stays with the body through the process, so the destination person is still you
the most powerful and dense thing in the universe; cannot be destroyed; carries all memories permanently
aliens already teleport — faster-than-light physical travel is impossible so they must use wormholes or teleportation
coming after teleportation; 'gods' in mythology and the Bible were likely time-traveling future humans
dismisses them as stupid people who parrot talking points and choose disbelief out of anger, not reason
technology manifests when society is open-minded and trusting enough to be ready for it
necessary prerequisite — closing borders reflects the distrust that blocks technological and civilizational progress
would not be an early beta tester but would use it once widely adopted, to avoid missing out on life
